reasoning: The focal event between March 15 and March 22, 2026, is the FOMC meeting on March 17-18. A majority of market commentary points toward the Fed holding interest rates (3.50%-3.75%) and potentially revising its "dot plot" downward to indicate fewer rate cuts in 2026. Such a hawkish stance generally asserts the Fed's credibility in controlling inflation, which historically places downward pressure on the 5-Year, 5-Year Forward Inflation Expectation Rate (T5YIFR). While the recent T5YIFR level of 2.11 is near the bottom of its immediate 1-month range (2.10-2.14), raising the likelihood of mean reversion, the broader linear trend over the last month projects a continued slight decrease (-0.0016 per day). Taking into account the overarching hawkish macroeconomic signals, the probability of a strict positive increase from 2.11 seems slightly below 50%.
